You’ll help these organizations scale effectively by strengthening leadership capability, shaping organizational design, and aligning talent strategy to the product strategy. You’ll also collaborate closely with PDE People Partners and other cross-functional partners across the People Team - including Compensation, Recruiting, Learning & Development, BEI, and People Relations - to drive high-impact initiatives that support the holistic Engineering and PDE organizational goals.</p> <p>This role offers the opportunity to shape how leadership, performance culture, and talent strategy evolve as Figma’s Product Engineering and Data organizations continue to grow and mature. </p> <p>This is a full-time role that can be based in one of our US hubs or remotely within the United States.</p> <h4>What you’ll do at Figma:</h4> <ul> <li>Partner closely with Product Engineering + Data leadership teams as a trusted advisor, providing strategic guidance on leadership effectiveness, organizational design, and talent strategy</li> <li>Support continued scaling of the Product Engineering & Data organizations by evolving team structures, roles, and operating models in line with boarder Engineering organizational growth and productivity goals</li> <li>Coach senior leaders to strengthen leadership capability, team effectiveness, and performance in a high-growth, fast-changing environment</li> <li>Partner with Product Engineering and Data Leadership to evolve performance and compensation frameworks, talent planning, and succession strategies that drive business outcomes and accountability</li> <li>Identify and address organizational friction points by improving cross-functional alignment and operational clarity across PDE</li> <li>Embed as a key partner across cross-functional stakeholders - including other Engineering and regional People Partners, Compensation, Recruiting, Learning & Development, BEI, and People Relations - to drive and deliver high-impact people initiatives.</li> <li>Leverage data, organizational insights, and employee feedback to proactively inform decisions and drive measurable improvements in team health, effectiveness, and performance</li> <li>Develop and drive people strategy in service of Engineering priorities, ensuring alignment with the broader PDE strategy</li> </ul> <div class="section page-centered"> <h4>We'd love to hear from you if you have:</h4> <ul> <li>8+ years in HR / People roles, including 5+ years as an HR Business Partner supporting senior leaders in a high-growth company</li> <li>Proven track record partnering with PDE clients, especially Engineering</li> <li>Strong background in organizational design, leadership coaching, and change management</li> <li>Demonstrated ability to influence senior leaders and drive alignment on complex, cross-functional initiatives in fast-paced, ambiguous environments</li> <li>Skilled at leveraging data and insights to inform decisions and translate business strategy into talent and organizational strategy</li> </ul> <h4>While not required, it’s an added plus if you also have:</h4> <ul> <li>Experience in high-growth SaaS or product-led companies</li> <li>Scaling organizations through rapid growth and transformation</li> <li>Partnering closely with executive leadership teams</li> </ul> <div class="section page-centered"> <div> <div>At Figma, one of our values is Grow as you go. From idea to product, Figma empowers teams to streamline workflows, move faster, and work together in real time from anywhere in the world. If you're excited to shape the future of design and collaboration, join us!</p></div><p>Figma is hiring for a Manager, Enterprise Sales to shape our sales strategy and collaborate with teams across the company. You aren’t the typical sales leader, and we aren’t the typical design tool company. At Figma we think creatively, lift constraints that block our imagination everyday and we expect you to do the same. With this role you’ll get in at the ground floor in a fast growing sales org. You’ll collaborate with our existing customer base and potential customers in driving meaningful relationships that make valuable contributions to the customer experience and revenue to our bottom line.</p> <p>Figma is growing and moving fast. Our next team member will have an agile, dynamic mentality, be comfortable embracing change, a true team-player, and love working with our growing footprint of Fortune 1000 customers. You won’t be alone! Figma has an incredible team and you’ll work closely with other groups including Support, Design, Engineering, Product Marketing, Customer Success, and Community. As a Manager, Enterprise Sales, you will lead a team of Account Executives responsible for the engagement, expansion and retention of our rapidly growing customer base. You will report to the Director of Enterprise Sales.</p> <p>This is a full time role that can be held from one of our US hubs or remotely in the United States.</p> <h4>What you’ll do at Figma:</h4> <ul> <li>Lead a team of motivated and diverse individuals to foster an environment of success</li> <li>Participate in customer and prospect meetings</li> <li>Assist in continual training and development, including mentorship, hiring and career growth</li> <li>Help shape and further develop sales processes and initiatives</li> <li>Interview, hire and train new team members as we scale the business and company</li> <li>Deliver weekly forecast meetings and advise Account Executives on strategies to help exceed their goals</li> </ul> <div class="section page-centered"> <h4>We'd love to hear from you if you have:</h4> <ul> <li>7+ years of SaaS sales experience, including 2+ years managing sales teams</li> <li>Strong understanding of technical products, market dynamics, and competitive landscapes</li> <li>Proven ability to thrive in fast-paced, constantly evolving environments</li> <li>Exceptional coaching, communication, discovery, and presentation skills</li> <li>Passion for driving customer success, building lasting relationships, and delivering consistent sales results</li> </ul> <div class="section page-centered"> <div> <div>At Figma, one of our values is Grow as you go. jobs.title: Recruiting Optimization Manager
jobs.description: About the Team OpenAI’s People team is committed to hiring, engaging, and supporting world-class talent to help safely build and deploy universally beneficial Artificial General Intelligence (AGI). The Recruiting Optimizations Manager is responsible for building and strengthening the systems, workflows, and operational infrastructure that make hiring at OpenAI fast, rigorous, and scalable. This role partners across recruiting, systems, and people teams to ensure recruiting foundations are efficient, reliable, auditable, and built to scale as OpenAI continues to grow rapidly and globally. This role leads Recruiting Operations optimization efforts across workflow design, operational automation, systems enablement, and scalable service operations. About the Role This role is focused on the systems and operational backbone of recruiting. The Recruiting Operations Manager will lead work across recruiting systems, workflow design, automation, process optimization, and data integrity to improve how hiring runs at scale. This role will translate business needs into durable operational solutions, drive improvements across the recruiting technology stack, and identify opportunities to reduce manual work through better tooling, automation, and process design. The right person will combine strong people management with systems thinking, operational rigor, and a practical approach to scaling high-quality recruiting infrastructure. This role is based in San Francisco, CA. We are not considering remote candidates at this time. In this role, you will: Lead and develop a high-performing Recruiting Operations team, setting clear expectations, building strong judgment, and supporting career growth. Own and improve the operational systems and workflows that underpin recruiting, with a focus on scalability, reliability, and efficiency. Design, implement, and continuously optimize automations across recruiting workflows, approvals, data quality checks, reporting processes, and systems handoffs. Identify manual, repetitive, or error-prone work and convert it into streamlined, governed, and scalable processes. Partner with Recruiting, People Systems, and cross-functional stakeholders to prioritize and deliver tooling improvements, workflow redesigns, and operational enhancements. Support the evolution of the recruiting technology stack by defining requirements, evaluating solutions, improving integrations, and driving adoption of new processes and tools. Use data to monitor process health, identify bottlenecks, and drive continuous improvement across operational performance and system effectiveness. Raise the bar on data accuracy, systems hygiene, documentation, and process discipline across recruiting operations. Act as an escalation point for complex operational issues, using structured problem-solving and sound judgment to resolve root causes. Contribute to an AI-enabled roadmap for recruiting operations, with a focus on practical automation, usability, governance, and measurable impact. You might thrive in this role if: You care deeply about building reliable systems and operational foundations that scale. You naturally look for ways to simplify, automate, and optimize workflows rather than maintain manual workarounds. You are comfortable operating in ambiguity and can bring structure to evolving processes and systems. You know how to lead a team through both day-to-day execution and longer-term systems improvement. You bring strong judgment and know when to escalate risks, challenge unclear requirements, or tighten a process that is not holding up. You balance speed with rigor and understand that operational quality is a trust function. You can zoom out to design scalable workflows and zoom in to debug process failures, systems gaps, or data issues. You are credible with stakeholders and able to build strong working relationships across recruiting, systems, analytics, finance, legal, and IT. What We’re Looking For: 8+ years of experience in recruiting operations, talent operations, people systems, or similar operational environments, with prior people management experience. Experience leading teams in fast-paced, high-growth environments with a strong focus on systems, process improvement, and operational execution. Strong operational design and process optimization skills, with experience scaling recruiting workflows and infrastructure. Demonstrated experience building or driving workflow automations, tooling enhancements, systems improvements, or operational redesigns in recruiting or related environments. Comfort working across ATS, workflow, integration, and support tooling; experience with platforms such as Ashby, Workday, Guide, or similar systems is a plus. Experience using metrics, service levels, and operational reviews to manage performance and uncover opportunities for optimization and automation. Strong cross-functional partnership skills, especially with Recruiting, People Systems, Analytics, Finance, Legal, and IT. Excellent judgment, prioritization, communication, and attention to detail in high-stakes environments. Interest in AI-enabled operations, with a pragmatic approach to adopting new tools and workflows. Compensation Range: $216K - $240K USD About OpenAI OpenAI is an AI research and deployment company dedicated to ensuring that general-purpose artificial intelligence benefits all of humanity. We push the boundaries of the capabilities of AI systems and seek to safely deploy them to the world through our products. AI is an extremely powerful tool that must be created with safety and human needs at its core, and to achieve our mission, we must encompass and value the many different perspectives, voices, and experiences that form the full spectrum of humanity. We are an equal opportunity employer, and we do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, age, veteran status, disability, genetic information, or other applicable legally protected characteristic. For additional information, please see OpenAI’s Affirmative Action and Equal Employment Opportunity Policy Statement . Background checks for applicants will be administered in accordance with applicable law, and qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment consistent with those laws, including the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers, and the California Fair Chance Act, for US-based candidates. For unincorporated Los Angeles County workers: we reasonably believe that criminal history may have a direct, adverse and negative relationship with the following job duties, potentially resulting in the withdrawal of a conditional offer of employment: protect computer hardware entrusted to you from theft, loss or damage; return all computer hardware in your possession (including the data contained therein) upon termination of employment or end of assignment; and maintain the confidentiality of proprietary, confidential, and non-public information. In addition, job duties require access to secure and protected information technology systems and related data security obligations. To notify OpenAI that you believe this job posting is non-compliant, please submit a report through this form . No response will be provided to inquiries unrelated to job posting compliance. We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with disabilities, and requests can be made via this link . OpenAI Global Applicant Privacy Policy At OpenAI, we believe artificial intelligence has the potential to help people solve immense global challenges, and we want the upside of AI to be widely shared. Join us in shaping the future of technology.

Figma’s platform helps teams bring ideas to life—whether you're brainstorming, creating a prototype, translating designs into code, or iterating with AI. This is a high-impact role with ownership over collections strategy, cross-functional collaboration, and process improvement initiatives across our >$1B annual collections book. You'll partner closely with Revenue, Sales, Finance, and an offshore collections team to drive cash flow outcomes and support a clean monthly close.</p> <p>The ideal candidate is hands-on, detail-oriented, and energized by the chance to bring AI and automation into a finance function.<br><br>This is a full time role that can be held from one of our US hubs or remotely in the United States.</p> <h4>What you’ll do at Figma:</h4> <ul> <li>      Own end-to-end collections operations for an assigned book of business: aging management, dunning, dispute resolution, and escalation strategy</li> <li>      Monitor and drive improvement in AR KPIs (DSO, ADD, CEI, % current, bad debt)</li> <li>      Partner with Revenue, Sales, CS, and Finance to resolve billing and payment issues</li> <li>      Support month-end and quarter-end close, ensuring AR-related activities land accurately and on time</li> <li>      Direct and mentor offshore collectors — set priorities, QA work, run cadence</li> <li>      Manage and improve cash application, refund, and write-off processes</li> <li>      Identify and implement automation and AI opportunities across the AR stack</li> <li>      Prepare reporting and provide insights to leadership on AR performance and cash flow trends</li> </ul> <div class="section page-centered"> <h4>We'd love to hear from you if you have:</h4> <ul> <li>      5+ years of experience in AR / Collections in a SaaS or high-growth environment</li> <li>      Strong understanding of Quote-to-Cash processes and hands-on experience with NetSuite, Stripe, Tesorio, or similar tools</li> <li>      Proven experience improving collections performance and driving cash flow outcomes and experience supporting month-end / quarter-end close</li> <li> required, it’s an added plus if you also have:</h4> <ul> <li>      Experience managing or mentoring offshore resources and implementing or optimizing AR tools and automation</li> <li>      Sales tax, This is a remote first role. </p> <p>You will partner closely with teams across the company and focus on systemic security improvements and risk reduction. You will also participate in operational security responsibilities like security reviews, consulting, vulnerability triage, and security incident response.</p> <p><strong>Examples of what you may work on across teams:</strong></p> <p><strong>AI Security</strong></p> <ul> <li>Perform technical security assessments, code audits, and design reviews for new AI infrastructure, platforms, and products.</li> <li>Design and develop technical solutions to secure AI models, tooling, debugging workflows, and data pipelines.</li> <li>Advocate for secure practices across Figma’s AI infrastructure, platforms, and data systems.</li> <li>Build the next generation of internal AI-powered access insights and security tooling.</li> <li>Help run penetration testing and offensive security exercises against Figma’s AI infrastructure, platforms, and products.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Platform Security</strong></p> <ul> <li>Perform technical security assessments, code audits, and design reviews for changes to Figma’s cloud and corporate infrastructure.</li> <li>Design and develop solutions to prevent or mitigate cloud and corporate security risks.</li> <li>Advocate for secure practices within Figma’s cloud and corporate infrastructure.</li> <li>Build platforms and tooling to detect and respond to infrastructure and corporate security threats.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Product Security</strong></p> <ul> <li>Perform technical security assessments, code audits, and design reviews for new product features.</li> <li>Design and develop solutions to prevent or mitigate product security vulnerabilities.</li> <li>Advocate for secure development practices across Figma’s products and services.</li> <li>Help run penetration testing, offensive security exercises, and support our bug bounty program.</li> <li>Help respond to product security incidents.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Anti-Abuse</strong></p> <ul> <li>Design and build technical systems to prevent spam, fraud, and abuse.</li> <li>Partner closely with product teams to identify and address potential abuse vectors.</li> <li>Develop new signals and improve the use of existing signals to detect abusive jobs.title: Product Manager, AI Growth
jobs.location: San Francisco, CA • New York, NY • United States
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Figma is growing our team of passionate creatives and builders on a mission to make design accessible to all. Figma’s platform helps teams bring ideas to life—whether you're brainstorming, creating a prototype, translating designs into code, or iterating with AI. From idea to product, Figma empowers teams to streamline workflows, move faster, and work together in real time from anywhere in the world. If you're excited to shape the future of design and collaboration, join us!</p></div><p>We’re looking for a strategic, entrepreneurial Product Manager to help shape product-led growth for our newest AI products. Figma has an ambitious, forward-looking roadmap that we’re rapidly executing on as product development and design rapidly change with AI. This role will help drive the company’s strategy for growth of these products and ensure users discover value in Figma as their workflows rapidly evolve. </p> <h2><strong>What you’ll do at Figma:</strong></h2> <ul> <li>Partner closely with other product teams to develop and execute on a strategy for driving adoption of Figma’s newest and biggest bets.</li> <li>Use data and insights to build a portfolio of experiments and new features that drive users to discover the transformative impact of Figma’s AI features and products</li> <li>Shape Figma’s growth strategy as our business and product evolve.</li> <li>Lead a fast-executing, impact-driven team. Collaborate deeply with Design, Engineering, and Product Leadership on both strategy and execution.</li> </ul> <h2><strong>We’d love to hear from you if you have:</strong></h2> <ul> <li>7+ years of product management experience</li> <li>Familiarity with current AI-powered product development and a POV on how our industry will evolve</li> <li>Experience leading major product or growth initiatives across user-facing products</li> <li>Track record of delivering impact through data-driven experimentation and strategic bets</li> <li>A knack for data and metrics, and strength in translating insights into strategy </li> <li>Are a strong cross-functional collaborator, both within the product development organization and with marketing / business functions</li> <li>Have deep user empathy and are able to understand complex product spaces</li> </ul> <div class="section page-centered"> <div> <div class="section page-centered"> <div> <div>At Figma, one of our values is Grow as you go. We believe in hiring smart, curious people who are excited to learn and develop their skills. If you’re excited about this role but your past experience doesn’t align perfectly with the points outlined in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyways. You may be just the right candidate for this or other roles.</div> </div> </div> </div> </div><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p><strong><span style="font-size: 16px;">Pay Transparency Disclosure</span></strong></p> <p>If based in Figma’s San Francisco or New York hub offices, this role has the annual base salary range stated below.    </p> <p>Job level and actual compensation will be decided based on factors including, but not limited to, individual qualifications objectively assessed during the interview process (including skills and prior relevant experience, potential impact, and scope of role), market demands, and specific work location. The listed range is a guideline, and the range for this role may be modified. For roles that are available to be filled remotely, the pay range is localized according to employee work location by a factor of between 80% and 100% of range. Please discuss your specific work location with your recruiter for more information. </p> <p>Figma offers equity to employees, as well a competitive package of additional benefits, including health, dental & vision, retirement with company contribution, parental leave & reproductive or family planning support, mental health & wellness benefits, generous PTO, company recharge days, a learning & development stipend, a work from home stipend, and cell phone reimbursement.  Figma also offers sales incentive pay for most sales roles and an annual bonus plan for eligible non-sales roles. Figma’s compensation and benefits are subject to change and may be modified in the future.</p></div><div class="title">Annual Base Salary Range:</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$207,000</span><span class="divider">—</span><span>$303,000 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><p>At Figma we celebrate and support our differences. We know employing a team rich in diverse thoughts, experiences, and opinions allows our employees, our product and our community to flourish. Figma is an <a href="https://www.eeoc.gov/sites/default/files/2022-10/EEOC_KnowYourRights_screen_reader_10_20.pdf">equal opportunity workplace</a> - we are dedicated to equal employment opportunities regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity/expression, veteran status<strong>, </strong>or any other characteristic protected by law. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements.</p> <p>We will work to ensure individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to apply for a role, participate in the interview process, perform essential job functions, and receive other benefits and privileges of employment. If you require accommodation, please reach out to <a href="mailto:accommodations-ext@figma.com">accommodations-ext@figma.com</a>. These modifications enable an individual with a disability to have an equal opportunity not only to get a job, but successfully perform their job tasks to the same extent as people without disabilities. </p> <p>Examples of accommodations include but are not limited to: </p> <ul> <li>Holding interviews in an accessible location</li> <li>Enabling closed captioning on video conferencing</li> <li>Ensuring all written communication be compatible with screen readers</li> <li>Changing the mode or format of interviews </li> </ul> <p>To ensure the integrity of our hiring process and facilitate a more personal connection, we require all candidates keep their cameras on during video interviews. Additionally, if hired you will be required to attend in person onboarding.</p> <p>By applying for this job, the candidate acknowledges and agrees that any personal data contained in their application or supporting materials will be processed in accordance with <a class="c-link c-link--underline" href="https://www.figma.com/legal/candidate-privacy-notice/" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="https://www.figma.com/legal/candidate-privacy-notice/" data-sk="tooltip_parent">Figma's Candidate Privacy Notice</a>.</p></div>

jobs.title: People (HR) Operations Specialist
jobs.description: About the Team The People Operations team at OpenAI is the engine that keeps our employee lifecycle running seamlessly, from a new hire’s first day to major organizational milestones. We partner across the company to deliver operational excellence in onboarding, employee mobility, M&A integrations, international expansion, audit/compliance, and beyond. Our work blends precision, adaptability, and innovation—whether we’re maintaining critical systems, optimizing processes, or building automations that scale with our rapid growth. We thrive in complexity, moving quickly to solve problems and anticipate needs before they arise. We value clarity over complexity, collaboration over silos, and are relentless in our focus on creating a smooth, efficient, and world-class employee experience. About the Role We’re seeking a proactive and adaptable professional who thrives at the intersection of HR Operations, Systems, and Compliance. In this role, you’ll be a key driver of ensuring employee data integrity through a suite of people data audits that span across the entire employee lifecycle. These audits will focus on Workday worker profile creation, job changes, one-time payments, and terminations. You’ll play a critical role supporting our diverse and fast-evolving needs related to ad-hoc reporting and establishing automated reconciliations against large data sets. You bring a sense of urgency, strong process mapping and operational design skills and effectively partner with engineering and systems teams to translate complex operational requirements into scalable, efficient workflows. If you enjoy working in a dynamic environment where no two days are the same and where your work directly fuels company growth and operational excellence, this role is for you. This role reports to the HR Ops Lead. In this role, you will: Maintain Workday data, and ensure that updates across the company are accurately reflected, including open positions, the creation of hire records, and internal mobility. Respond to inquiries from employees and cross functional partners to ensure a positive experience, with a commitment to accuracy, compliance, and efficiency. Support global employee onboarding, including the bespoke needs of executives and acquisitions while prioritizing timeliness and data accuracy. Partner closely with Program owners, Systems, Employment Legal, Compliance, Recruiting Ops, Payroll, Finance, HRBPs, IT, Workplace Operations, Corporate Security, and others. Collaborate across the spectrum of People processes to ensure a holistic, interconnected system that delivers excellence across the employee lifecycle. Continuously measure and act to elevate metrics relating to employees’ experience. Drive cross-functional collaboration with key stakeholders and partner teams, leveraging strong project management expertise to help lead large-scale, strategic workstreams and change management initiatives with significant operational impact. Act as a thought leader in leveraging AI and operational best practices to automate repetitive administrative work, improving efficiency and accuracy, while delivering a best-in-class employee experience. You might thrive in this role if you have: 5+ years of experience working in an HR or People Operations role through company growth and scaling, a resourceful approach, and ability to thrive in ambiguity. Proficiency with Workday and advanced spreadsheet analysis (e.g. utilize apps scripts). The ability to deconstruct business processes to their core components and identify root causes for gaps or inefficiencies. Fantastic communication and interpersonal skills with the ability to explain complex concepts in a simple way. Passion for enabling employee lifecycle moments that delight and building processes that scale; particularly using AI solutions. Unwavering attention to detail, exceptional rigor, and a commitment to unparalleled excellence in your craft. Workplace & Location This role is based in our San Francisco or NYC office, and we are not considering remote applications at this time. We do encourage you to apply even if you prefer a fully remote role as location factors may change over time. We offer relocation support to new employees, and we use a hybrid model: three days in the office per week with optional work from home on Thursdays and Fridays. Our open-plan offices have height-adjustable desks, conference rooms, phone booths, well-stocked kitchens full of snacks and drinks, three in-house prepared meals daily, a private outdoor space for working in the sun or socializing, nap rooms, private bike storage, and more. About OpenAI OpenAI is an AI research and deployment company dedicated to ensuring that general-purpose artificial intelligence benefits all of humanity. We push the boundaries of the capabilities of AI systems and seek to safely deploy them to the world through our products. AI is an extremely powerful tool that must be created with safety and human needs at its core, and to achieve our mission, we must encompass and value the many different perspectives, voices, and experiences that form the full spectrum of humanity. We are an equal opportunity employer, and we do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, age, veteran status, disability, genetic information, or other applicable legally protected characteristic. For additional information, please see OpenAI’s Affirmative Action and Equal Employment Opportunity Policy Statement . Background checks for applicants will be administered in accordance with applicable law, and qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment consistent with those laws, including the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers, and the California Fair Chance Act, for US-based candidates. For unincorporated Los Angeles County workers: we reasonably believe that criminal history may have a direct, adverse and negative relationship with the following job duties, potentially resulting in the withdrawal of a conditional offer of employment: protect computer hardware entrusted to you from theft, loss or damage; return all computer hardware in your possession (including the data contained therein) upon termination of employment or end of assignment; and maintain the confidentiality of proprietary, confidential, and non-public information. In addition, job duties require access to secure and protected information technology systems and related data security obligations. To notify OpenAI that you believe this job posting is non-compliant, please submit a report through this form . No response will be provided to inquiries unrelated to job posting compliance. We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with disabilities, and requests can be made via this link . OpenAI Global Applicant Privacy Policy At OpenAI, we believe artificial intelligence has the potential to help people solve immense global challenges, and we want the upside of AI to be widely shared. Join us in shaping the future of technology.

future of AI at Figma in collaboration with cross-functional teams of innovative software engineers, product managers, and data scientists</li> <li>Break down open-ended AI problems into engineering jobs.title: Account Executive, Growth - Figma Weave (New York, United States)
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Figma is growing our team of passionate creatives and builders on a mission to make design accessible to all. Figma’s platform helps teams bring ideas to life—whether you're brainstorming, creating a prototype, translating designs into code, or iterating with AI. From idea to product, Figma empowers teams to streamline workflows, move faster, and work together in real time from anywhere in the world. If you're excited to shape the future of design and collaboration, join us!</p></div><p>We’re building the next chapter of AI-native craft and creativity - where technology and human expression meet. Figma Weave is a unified platform that bridges the gap between AI capabilities and creative skill, letting professional designers and creators harness the world’s leading AI models without sacrificing precision, expression, or soul. We’re building tools that help people move faster without flattening creativity - a platform where AI amplifies craft instead of replacing it.</p> <p>We are looking for an Account Executive, Growth to own Figma Weave’s most important creative accounts globally- the film, tech, agencies, and consumer brands with the largest and most sophisticated creative organizations in the world. This is not a customer acquisition role. You will own a named book of large accounts, build deep executive relationships, drive organizational change, and be measured on your ability to expand Weave’s footprint across these accounts over time. You will be a trusted partner to the creative leaders shaping culture at the most influential companies in the world, and a critical voice back into Figma on what it takes to win at the highest level.</p> <p>This is a full time role based from Figma's NYC hub.</p> <h4><strong>What you’ll do at Figma Weave:</strong></h4> <ul> <li>Own a named book of the world’s most creatively-intensive large accounts - spanning entertainment, gaming, technology, consumer brands, and global creative networks—and be accountable for net revenue retention and expansion targets within that portfolio</li> <li>Build and advance executive-level relationships across each account - from Chief Creative Officers and VPs of Design to CTOs and CMOs - becoming a trusted advisor on how Figma Weave can transform how their organizations create</li> <li>Drive organizational change within accounts by identifying and developing internal champions, mapping power structures, and gaining executive sponsorship for Weave deployments at scale</li> <li>Develop deep, multi-year account plans that map each organization’s creative priorities to Figma Weave’s capabilities - identifying expansion opportunities across teams, geographies, and use cases, and multi-threading at an executive level to accelerate them</li> <li>Partner closely with Customer Enablement, Advocates, and Product Marketing to ensure customers realize measurable value from Weave, prevent contraction, and unlock new expansion opportunities within the account</li> <li>Represent the voice of your strategic accounts back into Figma - influencing Weave’s product roadmap and go-to-market strategy with first-hand insight from the most demanding creative organizations in the world</li> </ul> <h4><strong>We’d love to hear from you if you have:</strong></h4> <ul> <li>8+ years of with a proven track record managing and growing strategic or named enterprise accounts at a global level, with consistent performance exceeding net revenue retention and expansion targets</li> <li>Demonstrated experience building C-suite and VP-level relationships and navigating complex organizational structures to gain sponsorship for enterprise-wide deployments</li> <li>Experience successfully managing complex, multi-stakeholder deal cycles (6 months+), 6/7 figure deals, with large global organizations</li> <li>Strong business acumen - able to connect creative and operational workflow challenges to measurable business outcomes at the board and executive level</li> <li>A consultative, value-based selling approach with a structured methodology (e.g. MEDDIC, Command of the Message) and a clear process for creating and quantifying customer value</li> </ul> <h4><strong>While it’s not required, it’s an added plus if you also have:</strong></h4> <ul> <li>Experience selling into creatively-intensive industries such as entertainment, gaming, advertising, or consumer brands, with a deep understanding of how large creative teams operate</li> <li>Familiarity with AI-native workflows, creative tooling, or content production at scale</li> <li>Demonstrated ability to succeed in a changing environment and sell a new, emerging product category to sophisticated buyers</li> <li>Certified in deal qualification, strategic account management, or prospect discovery frameworks</li> </ul> <p>At Figma, one of our values is Grow as you go. We believe in hiring smart, curious people who are excited to learn and develop their skills. If you’re excited about this role but your past experience doesn’t align perfectly with the points outlined in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyways. You may be just the right candidate for this or other roles.</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p><strong><span style="font-size: 16px;">Pay Transparency Disclosure</span></strong></p> <p>If based in Figma’s San Francisco or New York hub offices, this role has the annual base salary range stated below.    </p> <p>Job level and actual compensation will be decided based on factors including, but not limited to, individual qualifications objectively assessed during the interview process (including skills and prior relevant experience, potential impact, and scope of role), market demands, and specific work location. The listed range is a guideline, and the range for this role may be modified. For roles that are available to be filled remotely, the pay range is localized according to employee work location by a factor of between 80% and 100% of range. Please discuss your specific work location with your recruiter for more information. </p> <p>Figma offers equity to employees, as well a competitive package of additional benefits, including health, dental & vision, retirement with company contribution, parental leave & reproductive or family planning support, mental health & wellness benefits, generous PTO, company recharge days, a learning & development stipend, a work from home stipend, and cell phone reimbursement.  Figma also offers sales incentive pay for most sales roles and an annual bonus plan for eligible non-sales roles. Figma’s compensation and benefits are subject to change and may be modified in the future.</p></div><div class="title">Annual Base Salary Range:</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$165,000</span><span class="divider">—</span><span>$190,000 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><p>At Figma we celebrate and support our differences. jobs.title: Technical & Competitive Sales Solutions Manager

jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Figma is growing our team of passionate creatives and builders on a mission to make design accessible to all. Figma’s platform helps teams bring ideas to life—whether you're brainstorming, creating a prototype, translating designs into code, or iterating with AI. From idea to product, Figma empowers teams to streamline workflows, move faster, and work together in real time from anywhere in the world. If you're excited to shape the future of design and collaboration, join us!</p></div><p>The Technical & Competitive Sales Solutions manager will shape how Figma positions our cross-product technical solutions, and differentiates from both established and emerging competitors across priority segments and markets. This role will define our technical narrative and solutions, and translate competitive insights into clear and differentiated positioning. Ultimately, this person will deliver high-impact field assets and enablement that drive pipeline growth and revenue. </p> <p>The role will partner closely with Sales Leadership, Product, Marketing, and Enablement to ensure technical and competitive insights inform GTM strategy, product direction, and field execution. The ideal candidate combines analytical rigor with exceptional storytelling and a strong understanding of enterprise sales motions.</p> <p>This is a high-visibility opportunity to build something new – you’ll elevate Figma’s technical and competitive capabilities, and strengthen our overall sales strategy in an increasingly dynamic market. </p> <p>This is a full time role that can be held from one of our US hubs or remotely in the United States.</p> <h4>What you’ll do at Figma:</h4> <ul> <li>Develop differentiated technical messaging and positioning to show Figma’s strengths across product, design, and engineering workflows, aligned to Figma’s sales narrative</li> <li>Collect and synthesize multi-source intelligence (customer and sales feedback, industry research, analyst insights, sales data, etc.) to identify actionable competitive trends and dynamics</li> <li>Create high-impact technical and competitive assets, including architectures, battlecards, objection-handling frameworks, customer-facing materials, and segment-specific guides</li> <li>Establish scalable intake channels for competitive and buyer feedback, along with a framework for distributing insights across product and GTM stakeholders to influence company strategies</li> <li>Partner with the product team to understand and influence Figma’s product roadmap, funneling buyer feedback to highlight emerging customer needs and identify opportunities for new features that would increase differentiation and product-market fit</li> </ul> <div class="section page-centered"> <h4>We'd love to hear from you if you have:</h4> <ul> <li>4+ years of experience in competitive intelligence, product marketing, sales, or a related field</li> <li>4+ years of either go-to-market or direct work experience in software engineering, product development, or a related technical field</li> <li>Process creation experience – developing new processes, optimizing existing ones, and influencing decisions without direct authority</li> <li>Strong analytical, critical thinking, and problem-solving skills, with the ability to synthesize and interpret data and information from multiple sources and present it in a clear jobs.title: People Partner (London, United Kingdom)
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Figma is growing our team of passionate creatives and builders on a mission to make design accessible to all. Figma’s platform helps teams bring ideas to life—whether you're brainstorming, creating a prototype, translating designs into code, or iterating with AI. From idea to product, Figma empowers teams to streamline workflows, move faster, and work together in real time from anywhere in the world. If you're excited to shape the future of design and collaboration, join us!</p></div><div class="section page-centered"> <div> <p>In this role, you'll serve as the dedicated People Partner for Figma's EMEA region, working closely with leaders across the regions (mostly GTM teams across Sales, Marketing, and Product Support) in the UK, France & Germany predominantly. You’ll help the organisation scale effectively by strengthening leadership capability, shaping organisational design, and aligning talent strategy to business priorities. You’ll also collaborate closely with cross-functional partners across the People Team, including functional People Partners, Compensation, Recruiting, Learning & Development, BEI, and People Relations, to drive high-impact initiatives that support the business. </p> <p>This role offers the opportunity to build and shape how people strategy, leadership culture, and talent practices evolve across multiple markets, and at a pivotal stage in the region's growth.</p> <p>This is a full-time role based in our London hub.</p> <h4><strong>What you'll do at Figma:</strong></h4> <ul> <li>Act as the primary People Partner presence for the EMEA - the first point of contact for leaders across levels, GTM functions, and markets, bringing local context & understanding, and regional business understanding to your partnerships</li> <li>Work closely with functional People Partners to support the continued scaling of the teams in region by evolving team structures, roles, and operating models in line with business growth. Serve as the regional connector between functional People Partners and regional business leaders  - translating global people priorities into locally relevant solutions</li> <li>Coach senior leaders to strengthen leadership capability, team effectiveness, and performance in a high-growth, target-driven environment</li> <li>Identify and address organisational friction points by improving cross-functional alignment and operational clarity across teams, proactively surface and embed regional context in your work with partners</li> <li>Embed as a key partner across cross-functional stakeholders, including functional People Partners, Compensation, Recruiting, Learning & Development, BEI, and People Relations, to drive and deliver high-impact people initiatives</li> <li>Leverage data, organisational insights, and employee feedback to proactively inform decisions and drive measurable improvements in team health, effectiveness, and performance</li> <li>Support in developing and driving an EMEA people strategy in line with regional GTM priorities and broader Figma people strategy</li> <li>Explore and embed AI tools and approaches that extend your reach and impact across the region - whether through smarter use of people analytics, scaled manager enablement, or more efficient ways of working - and share learnings with the broader People Partner team</li> </ul> <h4><strong>We’d love to hear from you if you have:</strong></h4> <ul> <li>8+ years of HR / People experience, including significant experience as an HR Business Partner supporting senior leaders in a high-growth company, we well as multi-jurisdiction EMEA experience - practical working knowledge of employment law, cultural norms, and people practices across multiple jurisdictions</li> <li>Generalist capability - demonstrated experience spanning Employee Relations & Operations, as well as strategic business partnering and partnering with GTM or revenue organisations (Sales, Marketing, and/or Product Support), with an understanding of go-to-market dynamics</li> <li>Strong understanding of cultural nuance and the ability to adapt your approach across the diverse markets across EMEA</li> <li>Demonstrated ability to influence senior leaders and drive alignment in fast-paced, ambiguous environments, including when operating remotely from CoEs</li> <li>Experience supporting globally distributed teams and working effectively in a shared People Partner model with counterparts in other regions</li> </ul> <h4><strong>While it’s not required, it’s an added plus if you also have:</strong></h4> <ul> <li>Experience in high-growth SaaS or product-led companies</li> <li>Fluency in additional languages (French, German)</li> </ul> <div class="section page-centered"> <div> <div>At Figma, one of our values is Grow as you go. We believe in hiring smart, curious people who are excited to learn and develop their skills. If you’re excited about this role but your past experience doesn’t align perfectly with the points outlined in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyways. You may be just the right candidate for this or other roles.</div> </div> </div> </div> </div><div class="content-conclusion"><p>At Figma we celebrate and support our differences. jobs.title: Program Manager, M&A Recruiting | People
jobs.description: About the Team The Strategic Initiatives team sits within OpenAI’s Recruiting org and focuses on some of the most dynamic, people-driven areas of the company. We lead efforts across Executive Recruiting, Mergers & Acquisitions, Talent Intelligence, High Impact Talent, and Special Projects. About the Role As a Mergers & Acquisitions Program Manager within Recruiting, you’ll be a key partner in managing people XFN from diligence through employee onboarding on M&A deals. This role demands exceptional judgment, speed, and absolute discretion. You’ll operate at the center of some of OpenAI’s most impactful decisions - shaping the future of the company by bringing in extraordinary, mission-aligned talent. We’re looking for someone who thrives in ambiguity, moves seamlessly between strategic thinking and execution, and is energized by complexity, challenge, and pace. This role is based in our San Francisco office and we aren’t considering remote applications at this time. We use a hybrid work model of 3 days in the office with optional work from home on Thursdays and Fridays. We also offer relocation assistance to new employees. Your Responsibilities Co-design, lead, and scale the M&A People XFN workstream program, driving end-to-end management of timeline, candidate experience, and key touchpoints that enable smooth deal execution and set the stage for positive onboarding and integration. Build and uplevel playbooks, processes, and communications to support increasing deal volume and complexity, creatively solving for new challenges while ensuring clarity, alignment, and efficiency across cross-functional stakeholders (ex: Recruiting Operations, Comms, Events, etc.) Deliver a high-impact candidate and acquired team experience, including producing events such as Town Halls, onboarding customizations, and cultural moments that reflect OpenAI’s mission. Partner closely with senior leaders, founders, and acquired teams, demonstrating executive presence and the ability to navigate sensitive situations and competing priorities. Lead post-mortem retrospectives for People XFN both at a deal and program level to build repeatable playbooks/process improvement for future deals. Liaise with Recruiting Operations and cross-functional partners to build and scale efficient M&A recruiting processes and tools. This may include initiatives related to headcount tracking and allocation, offer creation and issuance, confidentiality workflows, etc. Flex into other high-priority areas of Strategic Initiatives as needed. We're Seeking 6+ years of relevant industry experience, including at least 4 years leading program management efforts within Mergers & Acquisitions or driving complex, cross-functional initiatives within specialized technical environments. Strong project management skills, including leading initiatives from project scoping to execution phases and the ability to drive alignment across stakeholders, timelines, and tools. Strong analytical mindset, leveraging data to evaluate program health, identify risks early, and guide clear, informed decision-making. High ownership mindset with intellectual curiosity and enthusiasm for technology; thrives in complex, continuously challenging contexts. Proven success operating with discretion and high judgment on sensitive or high-profile hires. The ability to zoom out strategically and operate at high altitude, while maintaining precision in execution and attention to detail. Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to tailor messaging across diverse stakeholders. Nice to Have Familiarity with technical hiring, especially in research or AI domains. Background in recruiting, education program design, or talent development. Experience supporting or building community-based programs. Project Management certification (e.g., PMP or equivalent). About OpenAI OpenAI is an AI research and deployment company dedicated to ensuring that general-purpose artificial intelligence benefits all of humanity. We push the boundaries of the capabilities of AI systems and seek to safely deploy them to the world through our products. AI is an extremely powerful tool that must be created with safety and human needs at its core, and to achieve our mission, we must encompass and value the many different perspectives, voices, and experiences that form the full spectrum of humanity. We are an equal opportunity employer, and we do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, age, veteran status, disability, genetic information, or other applicable legally protected characteristic. For additional information, please see OpenAI’s Affirmative Action and Equal Employment Opportunity Policy Statement . Background checks for applicants will be administered in accordance with applicable law, and qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment consistent with those laws, including the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers, and the California Fair Chance Act, for US-based candidates. For unincorporated Los Angeles County workers: we reasonably believe that criminal history may have a direct, adverse and negative relationship with the following job duties, potentially resulting in the withdrawal of a conditional offer of employment: protect computer hardware entrusted to you from theft, loss or damage; return all computer hardware in your possession (including the data contained therein) upon termination of employment or end of assignment; and maintain the confidentiality of proprietary, confidential, and non-public information. In addition, job duties require access to secure and protected information technology systems and related data security obligations. To notify OpenAI that you believe this job posting is non-compliant, please submit a report through this form . No response will be provided to inquiries unrelated to job posting compliance. We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with disabilities, and requests can be made via this link . OpenAI Global Applicant Privacy Policy At OpenAI, we believe artificial intelligence has the potential to help people solve immense global challenges, and we want the upside of AI to be widely shared. Join us in shaping the future of technology.

jobs.title: Senior Counsel, Sanctions and Trade Compliance
jobs.description: About the Team OpenAI's Legal team plays a crucial role in furthering OpenAI's mission by tackling innovative, fundamental legal issues in AI. If you're passionate about doing significant and unique work in the legal and compliance field, this team is for you. The team comprises professionals from diverse fields, including technology, AI, privacy, IP, corporate, employment, tax law, regulatory, and litigation. About the Role As a member of our Security Legal team, you will be responsible for managing and evolving OpenAI’s sanctions compliance program, from developing an overall compliance strategy, mapping out screening architecture and workflow designs, and working cross functionally with colleagues on monitoring, metrics, and continuous improvement. You will manage sanctions compliance policy and strategy for one of the most closely watched and globally impactful technology platforms in the world, and have real ownership and the chance to develop systems that scale globally as the company and our technology continues to evolve. You’ll also collaborate on adjacent trade compliance and security issues. This role can be hybrid or remote and based in San Francisco, Washington, D.C., New York, or Seattle. We use a hybrid work model of 3 days in the office per week and offer relocation assistance to new employees. In this role you will: Advise cross-functional teams on compliance and legal requirements applicable to OpenAI’s products and services under U.S. and international sanctions regimes. Develop sanctions compliance strategies and processes at a global scale for a frontier AI company operating across jurisdictions and products. Partner closely with cross-functional teams across Finance, Integrity, GRC, and Engineering to implement sanctions screening and controls that are deeply embedded into business workflows. Own compliance documentation and reporting, including policies, standards, dashboards, metrics, and escalation frameworks to enable rapid monitoring, assessment, and improvement of compliance processes Work with cross-functional teams to deeply understand and integrate compliance systems and tools, ensuring compliance checks are seamless, reliable, and adaptable as OpenAI evolves. Lead ongoing reviews and risk assessments to ensure the continued effectiveness of our compliance controls and processes. Manage and coordinate vendors and other third parties who support OpenAI’s compliance operations. Assist in the development of trade compliance training materials for our cross-functional partners and business teams. Explore and deploy AI tools to enhance and streamline OpenAI’s trade compliance efforts. You might thrive in this role if you: Have 10+ years of experience in the trade compliance field, including developing and implementing trade compliance programs at a large, technology-focused company. Hold a J.D. from an accredited law school and maintain an active license to practice law, in good standing, in at least one U.S. jurisdiction. Bring a sophisticated understanding of how US and international sanctions laws apply to multinational US companies. Have successfully embedded comprehensive compliance programs and incorporated compliance processes into complex business workflows without slowing innovation. Enjoy learning new systems and becoming the go-to expert in the systems and technical tools required for your job. Are highly skilled at program management, including organizing complex systems, tasks, processes, and documentation. Communicate in a way that builds trust with cross-functional teams, from engineers to business leaders. Have a strong sense of ownership, are inquisitive and enthusiastic about technology, enjoy being continually challenged, and can demonstrate sound judgment in ambiguous situations. jobs.location: San Francisco, CA • New York, NY • United States

practices.</p> <p>This is a full time role that can be held from one of our US hubs or remotely in the United States.</p> <h4>What you’ll do at Figma:</h4> <ul> <li>Own end-to-end program execution for security, compliance, and infrastructure initiatives—managing dependencies, milestones, risks, and reporting at a portfolio level</li> <li>Lead highly cross-functional programs, using strong project management skills to deliver complex initiatives in a collaborative and transparent way</li> <li>Drive project alignment by handling dependencies, guiding technical discussions, facilitating jobs.title: Software Engineer, Code Platform
jobs.location: San Francisco, CA • New York, NY • United States
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Figma is growing our team of passionate creatives and builders on a mission to make design accessible to all. Figma’s platform helps teams bring ideas to life—whether you're brainstorming, creating a prototype, translating designs into code, or iterating with AI. From idea to product, Figma empowers teams to streamline workflows, move faster, and work together in real time from anywhere in the world. If you're excited to shape the future of design and collaboration, join us!</p></div><div class="section page-centered"> <p>Figma is a critical part of modern product development, where design and code are core primitives in how builders create, iterate, and ship products. Figma's Code Platform team builds the foundational infrastructure that enables seamless translation between design, code, and AI-powered creation. From LLM-powered code generation to agentic design-to-code workflows, we're integrating AI deeply into how developers experience Figma. Our work powers products such as Make, Prompt to Edit, MCP, and other Figma surfaces.</p> <p>Our mission is to power the interoperability of design ↔ code so it can be seamless and magical for our customers, while remaining ergonomic, scalable, and extendable for the company. As AI becomes central to how products are built in the industry, Code Platform sits at the intersection of design, engineering, and AI enabling the next generation of creation tools.</p> <p>We're hiring engineers to join Code Platform to work on our core code translation infrastructure. This role will focus primarily on deep technical execution, and depending on level and experience, may also include technical leadership responsibilities such as architectural ownership, mentoring, and driving multi-quarter initiatives.</p> <p>You'll build AI forward systems that translate between structured design models, and LLM-friendly representations, and balancing deterministic solutions with AI generated solutions across latency and correctness across Figma. This is a rare opportunity to improve and evolve one of the most strategically important abstractions in the company.</p> <p><strong>What you'll do at Figma:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Design, build, and improve code ↔ design translation pipelines, including AI-powered and agentic workflows</li> <li>Build and iterate on LLM integrations that power code generation, design interpretation, and agentic feature surfaces</li> <li>Design evaluation frameworks for AI-generated code outputs quality, correctness, and format fidelity</li> <li>Diagnose and resolve performance bottlenecks and long-running tasks that impact product SLAs</li> <li>Improve correctness, reconciliation, and fidelity across complex design system and layout edge cases</li> <li>Build scalable serialization pipelines, internal APIs, and platform utilities</li> <li>Partner with product leads to define long term product enablement and build foundational infra</li> <li>Improve observability and ownership of key metrics (latency, success rate, correctness)</li> <li>Support and drive architectural direction and long-term platform strategy for a translation surface</li> <li>Mentor engineers and shape the long-term team structure</li> </ul> <p><strong>We'd love to hear from you if you have:</strong></p> <ul> <li>5+ years of software engineering experience, especially in web, platform, or infrastructure engineering</li> <li>Strong TypeScript/JavaScript and modern frontend fundamentals</li> <li>Deep experience with declarative UI systems (React, JSX, or similar)</li> <li>Familiarity with ASTs, code transformation, or compilation concepts</li> <li>Experience building features that integrate LLMs or AI models into a product not just using AI tools, but shipping with them</li> <li>Experience debugging performance, rendering, or pipeline latency issues</li> <li>Experience building complex, cross-team platforms with multiple stakeholder groups</li> </ul> <p><strong>While it's not required, it's an added plus if you also have:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experience with prompt engineering, RAG patterns, or model evaluation</li> <li>Experience with code generation models or AST-level code manipulation</li> <li>Prior work on agentic workflows or multi-step LLM pipelines</li> <li>Experience with MCP, tool-use APIs, or AI developer ecosystems</li> <li>Experience building design systems or component libraries</li> <li>Platform or infrastructure engineering experience (APIs, observability, SLAs)</li> <li>Rendering, layout, or graphics pipeline knowledge</li> </ul> </div><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p><strong><span style="font-size: 16px;">Pay Transparency Disclosure</span></strong></p> <p>If based in Figma’s San Francisco or New York hub offices, this role has the annual base salary range stated below.    </p> <p>Job level and actual compensation will be decided based on factors including, but not limited to, individual qualifications objectively assessed during the interview process (including skills and prior relevant experience, potential impact, and scope of role), market demands, and specific work location. The listed range is a guideline, and the range for this role may be modified. jobs.location: San Francisco, CA • New York, NY • United States
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Figma is growing our team of passionate creatives and builders on a mission to make design accessible to all. Figma’s platform helps teams bring ideas to life—whether you're brainstorming, creating a prototype, translating designs into code, or iterating with AI. From idea to product, Figma empowers teams to streamline workflows, move faster, and work together in real time from anywhere in the world. If you're excited to shape the future of design and collaboration, join us!</p></div><div class="section page-centered"> <div class="section page-centered"> <p>Figma is growing our team of passionate creatives and builders on a mission to make design accessible to all. Figma’s platform helps teams bring ideas to life—whether you're brainstorming, creating a prototype, translating designs into code, or iterating with AI. From idea to product, Figma empowers teams to streamline workflows, move faster, and work together in real time from anywhere in the world. If you're excited to shape the future of design and collaboration, join us!</p> <p>Our AI Platform teams build the core frameworks and systems that support AI features across Figma, from infrastructure for training cutting-edge models to platforms for writing agentic product functionality.</p> <p>We’re hiring across several different teams:</p> <p><strong>Modeling Platform</strong></p> <p>The Modeling Platform team develops infrastructure for training, evaluating, deploying, and serving the AI models used across Figma. Modeling Platform engineers partner with our AI Research team to accelerate the end-to-end model development lifecycle, from experimentation to production.</p> <p><strong>Agent Platform</strong></p> <p>The Agent Platform team builds the frameworks and systems powering agentic AI features throughout Figma’s products. Agent Platform engineers partner with our product and infrastructure teams to unlock new AI capabilities, improve the development velocity of AI engineers, and ensure all of our AI features are reliable, performant, and high-quality.</p> <p><strong>Search & Recommendations</strong></p> <p>The Search & Recommendations team builds our platform for context-gathering for agents, surfacing content across the Figma ecosystem (text, design systems, and more) by combining traditional search, recommendation, and information retrieval infrastructure with modern agentic search capabilities. Search and Recommendations engineers partner with product teams across our AI organization to develop highly reliable and performant systems that improve AI quality.<br><br><strong>Make Platform</strong></p> <p>The Make Platform team builds systems that power a new way of creating and shipping software, where code, tooling, and runtime environments come together seamlessly. This includes standing up and managing sandboxed environments that can reliably run real applications, designing flexible pipelines that build and preview projects across a wide range of application frameworks, and integrating deeply with codebases to keep development state consistent as it evolves. The work spans distributed systems, developer infrastructure, and product-facing reliability challenges, with a focus on making complex workflows feel fast, intuitive, and dependable.</p> <p><strong>Extensibility</strong></p> <p>The Extensibility team is responsible for our plugin, widget, and REST APIs that enable developers to build their own Figma integrations. Extensibility engineers also partner with our product teams to develop our recently-launched MCP offerings and server platforms that integrate Figma into the wider AI ecosystem.<br><br><br></p> <p>We’re looking for engineers with a background in platform engineering or machine learning to strengthen our foundational AI systems & frameworks. As a member of our AI Platforms teams, you’ll play a critical role in developing cutting-edge AI models and new product functionality, while also upleveling the technical quality, performance, and reliability of our AI features. You’ll work alongside developers across the company, and combine industry best practices with a first-principle approach to design and build the platforms that power Figma’s design and collaboration products.</p> <p>This is a full time role that can be held from one of our US hubs or remotely in the United States.</p> <p><strong>What you’ll do at Figma:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Support end-to-end AI feature development by designing, building, and maintaining systems that are scalable, reliable, and performant.</li> <li>Collaborate with product, research, and infrastructure engineers working across AI.</li> <li>Help shape our platform strategy, roadmaps, and engineering best practices throughout the organization.</li> <li>Debug and resolve complex production issues that span services and layers of the stack.</li> <li>Mentor engineers and foster a culture of collaboration, inclusivity, and technical excellence.</li> </ul> <p><strong>We'd love to hear from you if you have:</strong></p> <ul> <li>5+ years of Software Engineering experience, especially in backend or infrastructure engineering.</li> <li>3+ years of experience in one or more of: AI, search infrastructure, or developer platforms.</li> <li>Proficiency in TypeScript, Python, or Ruby.</li> <li>Strong system design skills and a track record of architecting resilient production systems.</li> <li>Excellent communication skills, with experience collaborating across teams and mentoring others.</li> </ul> <p><strong>While not required, It’s an added plus if you also have:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Prior experience in building AI infrastructure at scale, whether that be model training or serving platforms.</li> <li>Experience evolving large-scale, shared developer platforms to improve reliability and developer velocity.</li> <li>A proven track record of leading technical strategy and complex, multi-team projects.</li> </ul> <p>At Figma, one of our values is Grow As You Go. We believe in hiring smart, curious people who are excited to learn and develop their skills. If you’re excited about this role but your past experience doesn’t align perfectly with the points outlined in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyways. jobs.title: Software Engineer, Graphics & Media
jobs.location: San Francisco, CA • New York, NY • United States
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Figma is growing our team of passionate creatives and builders on a mission to make design accessible to all. Figma’s platform helps teams bring ideas to life—whether you're brainstorming, creating a prototype, translating designs into code, or iterating with AI. From idea to product, Figma empowers teams to streamline workflows, move faster, and work together in real time from anywhere in the world. If you're excited to shape the future of design and collaboration, join us!</p></div><div class="section page-centered"> <p>Our Creation Engine teams work on some of the core technologies that power our real time, browser-based Figma Design and <a href="https://www.figma.com/figjam/">FigJam</a> products.  These teams work mostly (but not exclusively) on client-side code that runs in the browser.  Under the hood, Figma shares a lot of similarities to a game engine.  We develop this C++/WebAssembly engine to ensure internal and external developers can rapidly build new products and features that are fast and reliable by default, and own areas such as our in-memory data model (SceneGraph), renderer, and multiplayer client/server protocols.</p> <p>Within Creation Engine's Rendering & Animation org, the Pixel Engine team focuses on platform level work for a mix of rendering and media support at Figma. Pixel Engine works on the rendering engine, and builds new primitives and features to support new company efforts. </p> <p>The Pixel Engine team is looking for engineers with domain expertise in Media processing and playback. Our team is focused on a breadth of platform work, ranging from supporting new animation primitives, shader-based effects, and video and image import/export support. This specific role focuses on the Media components of Pixel Engine.</p> <p>This is a full-time role that can be held from one of our US hubs or remotely in the United States.</p> <h3><strong>What you’ll do at Figma:</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Work closely with teammates and partners in Engineering, Product, Design, Marketing, User Research, and Data Science to build new features and achieve roadmap goals. We prioritize collaboration and cross-functional partnerships, so you won’t operate in silos. Some efforts you might impact include:</li> <li>Supporting new client-side Media features (e.g. image processing pipelines)</li> <li>Improving the performance of media use cases (such as Image and Video) within the rendering engine</li> <li>End-to-end client support of new codecs/compression algorithms</li> <li>Error handling and network retry logicInterface with the rendering engine in scenarios of image and video rendering</li> <li>Drive guidance, quality, and standards for rendering at Figma, and explore opportunities that keep us on the bleeding edge of new capability and advancements.</li> <li>Be a mentor and provide thoughtful feedback to your peers, and benefit from strong personal and professional mentorship opportunities yourself.</li> <li>Contribute to org-wide initiatives like new hire onboarding, interview process development, and Belonging/Equity/Inclusion efforts.</li> <li>Continue ramping and learning. The technical challenges you'll tackle on the Pixel Engine team are unique, fun, ambitious, and ever-evolving.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>We'd love to hear from you if you have:</strong></h3> <ul> <li>5+ years of professional experience shipping software with a specialization on Video/Image rendering & playback.</li> <li>Experience with any of the following languages: C++, TypeScript, GLSL</li> <li>Experience working with web-based applications, browser performance, or real-time collaborative systems</li> <li>A track record of working collaboratively across teams and disciplines to deliver complex technical projects</li> </ul> <p>At Figma, one of our values is Grow as you go. We believe in hiring smart, curious people who are excited to learn and develop their skills. If you’re excited about this role but your past experience doesn’t align perfectly with the points outlined in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyways. jobs.location: San Francisco, CA • New York, NY • United States
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Figma is growing our team of passionate creatives and builders on a mission to make design accessible to all. Figma’s platform helps teams bring ideas to life—whether you're brainstorming, creating a prototype, translating designs into code, or iterating with AI. From idea to product, Figma empowers teams to streamline workflows, move faster, and work together in real time from anywhere in the world. If you're excited to shape the future of design and collaboration, join us!</p></div><p>We’re seeking an experienced Director, Technical Revenue to join our Revenue Accounting team. In this role, you’ll lead complex ASC 606 assessments, develop clear and comprehensive technical revenue memos, and collaborate with cross-functional partners to determine the appropriate revenue treatment for new products, pricing models, and commercial arrangements.</p> <p>You’ll play a meaningful role in shaping our revenue recognition policies and scaling our quote-to-cash systems as the business continues to grow.</p> <p>This is a full time role that can be held from one of our US hubs or remotely in the United States.</p> <h4>What you’ll do at Figma:</h4> <ul> <li>Own and evolve ASC 606 revenue policies for SaaS and consumption-based models, including credits, usage-based pricing, variable consideration, and contract modifications</li> <li>Lead technical accounting research and author clear, decision-ready memos, policies, and playbooks (e.g., SSP frameworks, non-standard deal guidance)</li> <li>Review complex enterprise and strategic contracts, advising Sales, Deal Desk, and Legal on structure, risk, and revenue implications</li> <li>Define revenue recognition approaches for new products and offerings, including performance obligations, SSP, principal vs. agent considerations, and contract changes</li> <li>Translate complex accounting guidance into practical recommendations and scalable processes for cross-functional partners</li> <li>Partner with auditors to support revenue positions, including leading position papers, walkthroughs, and evidence strategies, while ensuring SOX-compliant controls</li> <li>Build and develop a high-performing technical revenue team, coaching managers, and raising the bar on judgment, communication, and technical rigor</li> </ul> <div class="section page-centered"> <h4>We'd love to hear from you if you have:</h4> ASC 606</li> <li data-section-id="7j6ium" data-start="227" data-end="396">Strong command of US GAAP and SaaS revenue models, including multi-element arrangements, SSP, variable consideration, contract modifications, and usage-based pricing</li> <li data-section-id="1q3eo8h" data-start="397" data-end="517">Proven ability to evaluate complex contracts and determine appropriate revenue treatment across a range of scenarios</li> <li data-section-id="vy1zoj" data-start="518" data-end="631">Track record of influencing and defending accounting positions with external auditors and senior stakeholders</li> <li data-section-id="1md4kmg" data-start="632" data-end="747">Skilled at translating complex accounting concepts into clear, actionable guidance for non-accounting audiences</li> </ul> <h4>While not required, it’s an added plus if you also have:</h4> <ul> <li data-section-id="1otwuor" data-start="103" data-end="230">Background in public accounting (e.g., Big 4) combined with in-house experience at a high-growth SaaS or technology company</li> <li data-section-id="1dgt819" data-start="231" data-end="308">Familiarity with consumption-based or usage-based revenue models at scale</li> <li data-section-id="18iyhmx" data-start="309" data-end="400">Comfort operating in fast-paced environments with evolving products and business models</li> <li data-section-id="12ptk3o" data-start="401" data-end="475">Experience leading managers and developing technical accounting talent</li> </ul> <div class="section page-centered"> <div> <div>At Figma, one of our values is Grow as
